purpose: To explain how data moves through layers in computer networks in an interview-friendly way

OSI_Model.mnemonic: All People Seem To Need Data Processing

layers.function: Provides network services directly to the user or application
layers.interviewExplanation: This is the layer closest to the user. It allows applications like browsers and email clients to interact with the network.

layers.function: Handles data format, encryption, and compression
layers.interviewExplanation: This layer makes sure data is in a readable format. It encrypts and decrypts data for secure communication.

layers.function: Establishes, manages, and terminates communication sessions
layers.interviewExplanation: This layer manages sessions between applications and ensures they stay synchronized.

layers.function: Provides end-to-end communication, reliability, and flow control
layers.interviewExplanation: This layer decides whether data transfer should be reliable (TCP) or fast (UDP). It uses port numbers.
This layer decides the path data takes across networks using IP addresses.

layers.function: Provides MAC addressing and error detection
layers.interviewExplanation: This layer ensures data delivery within the local network using MAC addresses.

layers.function: Transmits raw bits over physical medium
layers.interviewExplanation: This layer deals with hardware transmission of bits as electrical or optical signals.

layers.role: Provides services like web, email, file transfer
Handles MAC addressing and physical transmission

threeWayHandshake.step1: Client sends SYN to start connection
threeWayHandshake.step3: Client sends ACK and connection is established

TCP_Details.whenUsed: File transfer, emails, web pages where accuracy matters

interviewSummary.keyPoint1: OSI is a reference model; TCP/IP is an implementation model
interviewSummary.keyPoint2: TCP works at Transport layer and ensures reliability
interviewSummary.keyPoint3: OSI helps in learning and troubleshooting networks
